pr.title: [claude] add when-blocked runbook
pr.body:
## What

Adds `docs/when-blocked.md` — a plain-language runbook for the moment a non-technical user's AI agent actually gets blocked by Reins. Also adds a one-line "what's here today" pointer in the README so the doc is discoverable.

## Why

The target structure in the repo brief calls out a `docs/when-blocked.md` runbook, and it is the single highest-leverage doc to ship first: it covers the moment of greatest user pain (something just stopped working) for the audience we are targeting (ops / compliance / IC users who do not run agents from a terminal). Every other doc — onboarding, admin rollout, per-runtime sub-skills — will eventually link here.

## Approach

The doc walks the reader through three real paths:

1. **Rephrase the request** (the safe-alternative path the agent itself surfaces — most blocks land here).
2. **Approve with a `CONFIRM-` token** (the override flow for blocks that are legitimate but high-risk).
3. **Escalate via the audit log** (when the block is `CRITICAL` and unbypassable, or the rule itself seems wrong).

Everything is grounded in the upstream Reins skill at `plugin/skills/reins/SKILL.md` and in `src/core/Arbitrator.ts` for the token format. Where a fact came from a specific upstream file, the doc links to it inline so a reader can verify.

Includes a small glossary at the bottom so the doc does not introduce a term it does not define (agent, hook, MCP, policy, audit log, Reins Cloud, severity labels).

## Non-goals for this PR

- Per-runtime nuance (how a block shows up specifically inside OpenClaw vs Hermes vs Replit Agent) — that belongs in the per-runtime sub-skills, which are separate PRs.
- An admin-side rollout doc — also a separate PR; this one only references it as a forward link.

## Review checklist

- [ ] Reads correctly to someone who does not know what a hook is.
- [ ] Every rule mentioned is traceable back to `pegasi-ai/reins` (no invented behavior).
- [ ] The three-path structure matches how Reins actually surfaces blocks.
- [ ] No jargon introduced without a one-line definition either inline or in the glossary.

Awaiting review before merge.

---
